# Constitution of the Cayman Islands - Quick Reference

> constitution.ky
> Interactive digital edition of the Cayman Islands Constitution Order 2009
> 9 parts, 125 sections, English

## Parts
I. Bill of Rights, Freedoms and Responsibilities (Sec. 1-28) - right to life, fair trial, expression, assembly, movement, non-discrimination, children, environment
II. The Governor (Sec. 29-42) - office, oaths, functions, deputy governor, pardon, public seal
III. The Executive (Sec. 43-58) - Cabinet, Premier, Ministers, Attorney General, National Security Council
IV. The Legislature (Sec. 59-93) - Legislative Assembly, elections, Speaker, bills, referendums, electoral boundaries
V. The Judicature (Sec. 94-107) - Grand Court, Court of Appeal, judges, Judicial and Legal Services Commission
VI. The Public Service (Sec. 108-110) - duty of officers, appointments, pensions
VII. Finance (Sec. 111-115) - revenue, reporting, public debt, Auditor General, Financial Secretary
VIII. Institutions Supporting Democracy (Sec. 116-122) - Human Rights Commission, Standards Commission, Constitutional Commission, Ombudsman, Freedom of Information
IX. Miscellaneous (Sec. 123-125) - official language (English), interpretation, reserved powers

## Key Sections
- Sec. 1: Bill of Rights is a cornerstone of democracy in the Cayman Islands
- Sec. 7: Right to a fair trial
- Sec. 16: Non-discrimination
- Sec. 43: Executive authority vests in Her Majesty
- Sec. 59: Legislature consists of Her Majesty and Legislative Assembly
- Sec. 94: Grand Court is a superior Court of Record
- Sec. 116: Human Rights Commission
- Sec. 123: Official language is English

## Full text available at /llms-full.txt
